title: Orthogonal Control — Imminent Submission
summary: A negative-control event: a single graded assignment is 90% done and due in 30 minutes, with a one-click submission once finished. There is no value clash (nothing social on offer), no interpersonal friction (no one to negotiate with), and no now-vs-later tension (the deadline is now). Every persona should converge on the same action — finish and submit. Used to validate the measurement: if behavioural divergence across personas is high HERE, the divergence metric is picking up noise/format, not persona. The expected reading is divergence ~ floor for all axes.

trigger.precondition: single dominant deadline, no competing options

tasks.label: Graded assignment — 90% done, submit when finished

visible_information.content: Assignment due Mon 13:30. Yours is 90% complete; submit once finished.
Only ~10% of the assignment remains; nothing else is time-sensitive.

context_switching_indicators: Reasoning recognises the assignment is the only thing that matters right now
efficiency_indicators: Finishes the assignment promptly rather than touching the filler task
evaluation.good_response_exemplar: Every persona finishes and submits the assignment first.
evaluation.bad_response_exemplar: Spending the window on the filler task or waiting.
